message:
type: PROPOSE
my_files: outlines/prompts.py
note: My feature: global filter registry (register_filter/unregister_filter) in outlines/prompts.py. What file(s) does your feature touch? If it's also prompts.py we need to agree on non-overlapping sections or sequence our edits.

type: PROPOSE
my_files: outlines/prompts.py
note: Adding custom filters param to prompt decorator, from_str/from_file, and create_jinja_env helper in outlines/prompts.py

type: REVISE
my_files: outlines/prompts.py
your_files: outlines/prompts.py
note: Same file, overlapping area (jinja env creation + filter registration) so true disjoint split isn't possible - let's agree on an interface instead. I will add a create_jinja_env(filters: Optional[Dict[str, Callable]] = None) -> jinja2.Environment helper that centralizes env creation (trim_blocks/lstrip_blocks/keep_trailing_newline/StrictUndefined) and registers the builtin filters (name/description/source/signature/schema/args) plus any passed-in filters dict. I'll use it from Prompt._template_from_str/_template_from_file and add a filters= kwarg to the prompt decorator (supporting @prompt and @prompt(filters={...}) usage) and to Prompt.from_str/from_file. Please have your global registry (register_filter/unregister_filter) apply its filters inside/via that same create_jinja_env() helper (e.g. merge registry filters with the explicit filters dict there) so the two features compose instead of each creating separate jinja2.Environment instances. Reply ACCEPT if that works for you, or REVISE with a different helper name/signature.

type: REVISE
my_files: outlines/prompts.py
your_files: outlines/prompts.py
note: We both only touch outlines/prompts.py per our task specs, so a disjoint file split isn't possible. Proposal: you implement your part first (filters param on prompt()/from_str()/from_file() + create_jinja_env(filters) helper) and send DONE with the exact signature of create_jinja_env and where it's called from. I'll then layer register_filter()/unregister_filter() + a module-level _FILTERS dict on top, editing create_jinja_env so it merges global _FILTERS with the local filters dict (local wins on name collision). I will not touch the prompt()/from_str()/from_file() signatures myself. Sound good?

type: ACCEPT
my_files: outlines/prompts.py
your_files: outlines/prompts.py
note: Agreed. I'll implement filters param on prompt()/from_str()/from_file() plus create_jinja_env(filters: Optional[Dict[str, Callable]] = None) -> jinja2.Environment, called from _template_from_str and _template_from_file. I will not touch _FILTERS/register_filter/unregister_filter. Will send DONE with exact final signature/call sites when finished.

type: ACCEPT
my_files: outlines/prompts.py
your_files: outlines/prompts.py
note: Accepted. I will add module-level _FILTERS dict + register_filter(name, func)/unregister_filter(name) in outlines/prompts.py, and merge _FILTERS into create_jinja_env() (global filters applied first, then explicit filters dict overrides on name collision so local wins). I will not touch prompt()/from_str()/from_file() signatures. Waiting for your DONE before I wire into create_jinja_env's body.

type: DONE
my_files: outlines/prompts.py
note: Finished. Final signature: create_jinja_env(loader: Optional[jinja2.BaseLoader], filters: Optional[Dict[str, Callable]] = None) -> jinja2.Environment. It builds the jinja2.Environment (loader=loader, trim_blocks/lstrip_blocks/keep_trailing_newline/StrictUndefined), registers builtin filters (name/description/source/signature/schema/args) on env.filters, then does env.filters.update(filters) if filters is not None (local filters win on name collision - keep this behavior when merging in your global _FILTERS, i.e. global first then local on top). Called from Prompt._template_from_str(_, content, filters=None) as create_jinja_env(None, filters), and from Prompt._template_from_file(_, path, filters=None) as create_jinja_env(jinja2.FileSystemLoader(file_directory), filters). prompt() is now prompt(fn: Optional[Callable] = None, *, filters: Optional[Dict[str, Callable]] = None) supporting both @prompt and @prompt(filters={...}) via an inner decorator() closure. Prompt.from_str/from_file also gained a filters= kwarg. All existing tests in tests/test_prompts.py pass. Feel free to add global-registry merging inside create_jinja_env - go ahead and edit it, that part is free for you now.

type: DONE
my_files: outlines/prompts.py
note: Finished. Added module-level _FILTERS: Dict[str, Callable] = {} plus register_filter(name, func) / unregister_filter(name) (placed right before def prompt(fn)). In my copy of the base file (without your create_jinja_env yet) I added 'env.filters.update(_FILTERS)' as the first filters call in both _template_from_str and _template_from_file, i.e. right after jinja2.Environment(...) is constructed and before the builtin filters/explicit filters are set - so global filters apply first and anything explicit (builtins or your local filters= dict) overrides them on name collision, matching what we agreed. To reconcile: inside your create_jinja_env(loader, filters), add 'env.filters.update(_FILTERS)' as the very first line after creating env, before setting the builtin name/description/source/signature/schema/args filters and before 'if filters is not None: env.filters.update(filters)'. That preserves precedence order: builtins/explicit filters > global registry filters, exactly as spec'd ("local filters taking precedence over global ones"). Tests in tests/test_prompts.py pass on my side. Ready to merge whenever you are.
